Table of Contents

NAME

SYNOPSIS

DESCRIPTION

The file specifies ‘‘netgroups’’, which are sets of tuples that are to be given similar network access. Each line in the file consists of a netgroup name followed by a list of the members of the netgroup. Each member can be either the name of another netgroup or a specification of a tuple as follows: (host, user, domain) where the and are character string names for the corresponding component. Any of the comma separated fields may be empty to specify a ‘‘wildcard’’ value or may consist of the string ‘‘-’’ to specify ‘‘no valid value’’. The members of the list may be separated by whitespace; the ‘‘\’’ character may be used at the end of a line to specify line continuation. The functions specified in should normally be used to access the database. These functions operate on the version of the file which can be generated using If that file is not present, and the system supports then the maps are used. The maps are also used if the file contains a entry. Lines that begin with a # are treated as comments.

FILES

the netgroup database.

SEE ALSO

COMPATIBILITY

The file format is compatible with that of various vendors, however it appears that not all vendors use an identical format.

BUGS

The interpretation of access restrictions based on the member tuples of a netgroup is left up to the various network applications.
